paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: Among spices analyzed, Cardamom and Nutmeg & Mace face negative overall growth in global markets, making them structurally declining categories globally. Clove is in a slightly better position but still shows slow growth on the world market alongside a low global volume — essentially a small, stagnant category. Sri Lanka's export data adds more nuance: Cardamom exports from Sri Lanka are actually growing (likely capturing market share from competitors even in a declining market), while Clove exports are not growing. By contrast, Cinnamon and Pepper — Sri Lanka's two largest spice exports — are both growing globally, with Cinnamon ranking among the fastest-growing products worldwide. This divergence suggests Sri Lanka should double down on Cinnamon and Pepper while carefully monitoring whether Cardamom's export growth can be sustained against a negative global demand backdrop.
supporting_quotes: Cardamom, Nutmeg & Mace has negative overall growth in global markets.
Clove is growing slowly on the world market while also having a low global volume.
Cardamom exports are growing; Clove exports are not.
Largest exports are Cinnamon and Pepper. Both are growing, but Cinnamon is growing faster.
Shrimp, Pepper, Mango, Vanilla, Ginger & Cinnamon have the highest growth rates
